title: Total Outage Engineering Runbook
content: A total outage affecting a service region is SEV1 and starts a 15-minute response SLA clock. RouterExecutor maps total-outage indicators deterministically; ActionAgent may search this runbook only after OPEN_INCIDENT. Engineering validates telemetry, identifies affected feeders or services, and publishes confirmed facts for customer-comms. During a surge, duplicate outage signals should attach as reports to the existing incident when dedup similarity meets the threshold. Knowledge search is skipped on ATTACH_TO_INCIDENT to keep surge latency flat.
snippet: Engineering runbook for total outages, SEV1 SLA, surge dedup, and confirmed-fact publishing.

title: Partial Outage Triage Procedure
content: Partial outage signals usually map to SEV2 when public-facing impact is spreading and SEV3 when contained to a single party. Capture affected system, location, start time, and magnitude from attached reports. Engineering compares telemetry with inbound signals and publishes source records before customer-comms sends restoration statements. If life-safety indicators appear, escalate regardless of partial scope. Every sitrep claim about affected area, magnitude, or ETA must cite a telemetry source, signal, or runbook record.
snippet: Procedure for partial outage severity, telemetry confirmation, and citation requirements.

title: Surge Dedup Knowledge Search Rule
content: During a surge, many signals describe the same real-world incident. RouterExecutor performs embedding-similarity dedup against open incidents in the same intent_category. At or above DEDUP_THRESHOLD, the signal becomes a report, magnitude increments, and the response is a short acknowledgment without knowledge search. Below threshold, RouterExecutor returns OPEN_INCIDENT and ActionAgent runs create_incident, search_knowledge, and generate_sitrep. This rule preserves every signal while keeping surge latency flat.
snippet: Explains ATTACH_TO_INCIDENT versus OPEN_INCIDENT behavior during a surge.
